pi_items.source: Found in export_result.sh which explicitly saves the JSON dictionary to this file path, and in verifier.py which reads from it.
The export script requests only the "name" field for locations. Odoo's short name matches exactly with these metadata strings, and the verifier successfully uses them to match short names.

Odoo's XML-RPC search_read returns Many2one fields as [ID, display_name]. For stock.location, the display_name is its complete_name which prepends the parent's complete_name. Since the parent is WH/Stock, the actual string in rule_loc_out[1] will be "WH/Stock/Zone A - Power Tools", explicitly contradicting the verifier's strict equality match against the short name.
Explicitly defined and used as the threshold integer inside verifier.py logic.

privileged_info_summary: The Odoo database is synthetically populated with specific product categories ("Power Tools", "Hand Tools", "Fasteners") and products via setup scripts. The export script correctly targets expected sub-locations by querying their short names. However, the task verifier contains a fatal flaw regarding the "expected_rules": it strictly compares Odoo's many2one location_out_id display_name against the short location names. Because Odoo automatically prepends the parent location path, the actual exported display names will be formatted as "WH/Stock/Zone A - Power Tools", meaning any agent that correctly sets WH/Stock as the parent location will automatically fail the rules verification check.
